subject: Natural Sciences
title: Forces in Action
tagline: Explore gravity, friction, magnetism and balanced/unbalanced forces!

stages.name: Stage 1
stages.question: What is a force?
options: It stays still or moves at constant speed
A push or pull on an object
stages.correctOption: A push or pull on an object
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A push or pull on an object is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A push or pull on an object.

stages.name: Stage 2
stages.question: What force pulls objects towards the Earth?
options: It stays still or moves at constant speed
The force of gravity acting on an object's mass
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Gravity.

stages.name: Stage 3
stages.question: What is friction?
options: The force of gravity acting on an object's mass
A push or pull on an object
A force that resists motion between surfaces
stages.correctOption: A force that resists motion between surfaces
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A force that resists motion between surfaces is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A force that resists motion between surfaces.

stages.name: Stage 4
stages.question: What unit is force measured in?
options: It stays still or moves at constant speed
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Newtons.

stages.name: Stage 5
stages.question: What happens when balanced forces act on an object?
options: A force that resists motion between surfaces
It stays still or moves at constant speed
stages.correctOption: It stays still or moves at constant speed
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! It stays still or moves at constant speed is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is It stays still or moves at constant speed.

stages.name: Stage 6
stages.question: What happens when unbalanced forces act on an object?
options: It stays still or moves at constant speed
The force of gravity acting on an object's mass
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! It accelerates or changes direction is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is It accelerates or changes direction.

stages.name: Stage 7
stages.question: What is 'mass'?
options: A force that resists motion between surfaces
The amount of matter in an object
stages.correctOption: The amount of matter in an object
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The amount of matter in an object is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The amount of matter in an object.

stages.name: Stage 8
stages.question: What is 'weight'?
options: The force of gravity acting on an object's mass
A force that resists motion between surfaces
stages.correctOption: The force of gravity acting on an object's mass
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The force of gravity acting on an object's mass is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The force of gravity acting on an object's mass.

stages.name: Stage 9
stages.question: Who discovered the laws of motion?
options: The amount of matter in an object
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Isaac Newton is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Isaac Newton.

stages.name: Stage 10
stages.question: What force allows a rocket to lift off?
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Thrust.
